meaning: n.实证哲学家,实证主义者
examples: If you are a "positivist" or "think positively" and also hold a non-dualistic philosophy, these two paths seem to conflict with each other.
In discussing means and ends, as well as facts and values, Simon (1947) is forthcoming concerning his own (logical positivist) philosophical perspective.
The view of education based on the positivist knowledge theory makes us only focus on knowledge instead of belief and morality while constructing our education ideal.
Ayer, the infamous logical positivist from Oxford who had arrived to run it.
